concepts.title: Importance of Women's Participation in Sports
concepts.description: Women's participation in sports is essential for their overall development, empowerment, and equality. It helps to break stereotypes, promotes healthy lifestyles, and provides opportunities for personal growth and achievement.

concepts.title: Objectives of Women and Sports
concepts.description: According to the NCERT textbook, the objectives of women and sports include improving physical and mental health, developing self-esteem and confidence, and promoting social and economic development.

concepts.title: Benefits of Sports for Children
concepts.description: The benefits of sports for children include developing physical fitness, improving mental health, enhancing social skills, and promoting emotional well-being. These benefits are often asked in the board exams, so it's essential to remember them.

concepts.title: Key Factors Affecting Women's Participation in Sports
concepts.description: To remember the key factors affecting women's participation in sports, use the mnemonic 'SPACE': S - Social barriers, P - Physical limitations, A - Access to facilities, C - Cultural norms, E - Economic constraints.

concepts.title: Confusing Equality with Equity in Sports
concepts.description: Students often confuse equality with equity in sports. Equality means providing the same opportunities and resources to everyone, while equity means providing fair and just opportunities and resources based on individual needs and circumstances. Make sure to understand the difference between these two concepts.

concepts.title: International Organisations for Women and Sports
concepts.description: Remember the following international organisations that promote women and sports: IOC (International Olympic Committee), UNESCO (United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization), and IWG (International Working Group on Women and Sport).

concepts.title: Role of Family and Community in Promoting Women's Sports
concepts.description: Family and community play a crucial role in promoting women's sports by providing support, encouragement, and access to resources and facilities. Their involvement is essential for overcoming social and cultural barriers that prevent women from participating in sports.

concepts.title: Government Initiatives for Women and Sports
concepts.description: The government has launched several initiatives to promote women and sports, including the 'Khelo India' programme, 'Sports for All' scheme, and 'National Sports Policy'. These initiatives aim to increase women's participation in sports, improve sports infrastructure, and provide training and coaching facilities.
